title: TR-MRV-Bench: a public per-facility emissions benchmark for Turkish CBAM-scope industry
description:
TR-MRV-Bench is a public per-facility CO₂ emissions benchmark for Turkish CBAM-scope industry (cement, steel, aluminum, fertilizer). It covers 59 facilities (34 cement, 16 steel, 6 fertilizer, 3 aluminum), with 21 audit-grade Scope 1 strong labels from operator Integrated Annual Reports, sustainability statements (GRI pre-2024, TSRS-compliant from CY 2024 under the KGK mandate effective 1 January 2024), and ISO 14064-1 audit reports issued by TÜRKAK-accredited verifiers.

Alongside the bench we release a closed-form physics baseline — tCO₂ = capacity × route-EF × capacity-factor — that reduces per-plant log-MAE by 82.3% against the EU CBAM default, measured leave-one-plant-out (each plant's route emission-factor is derived only from the other audit-grade plants, so no plant sees its own answer) over the 19 validatable plants. Two of the 21 audit-grade plants — BAGFAŞ (the sole N₂O-controlled fertilizer plant) and Gübretaş (the sole blender) — are each the only plant of their route and cannot be validated leave-one-plant-out, so they are excluded from the headline. The in-sample fit (emission-factor fitted on all plants, no hold-out) is 85.7%; the honest no-peeking number is 82.3%.

External validity (Verifier B6): applying the same formula with no re-tuning to EUTL third-party-verified Scope 1 for 789 EU plants across all four CBAM scopes (372 cement clinker installations, 282 pig-iron-and-steel, 71 aluminum, 64 ammonia + nitric-acid; 10,691 facility-years) gives a median ratio ≈1.0 vs the EU default's ≈2.5× on cement. An optional in-browser WebGPU model (iz) is shipped as an engineering demo only — at this data scale it does not beat the closed-form formula, so it is not part of the headline result.

The bench was the empirical basis for a formal submission to the European Commission's CBAM default-values implementing-act consultation (May/June 2026), proposing audit-anchored Turkey-specific defaults per CBAM route in place of the 90th-percentile-of-global anchor. Live site: https://iz-b0n.pages.dev . Source: https://github.com/abgnydn/iz . License: Apache-2.0.
notes: v0.4 release: honest leave-one-plant-out evaluation (+82.3%, n=19 validatable) supersedes the earlier in-sample +85.3% figure; EUTL external validation (Verifier B6) retained. The neural-net model (iz) is now shipped as an in-browser demo, not a headline result. The earlier PySR symbolic-regression claim (Verifier B7) has been withdrawn as circular — it recovered the identity it was given, not independent evidence. Apache-2.0 throughout; data, code, paper, and policy artifacts bundled.
